title: Property editor + constraint panel components
analysis: Task 91 creates four UI panel components for the Reify GUI: PropertyEditor, ConstraintPanel, Toolbar, and StatusBar. The codebase already has a solid foundation: types.ts defines ValueData (cell_id, name, value, unit, determinacy, entity_path) and ConstraintData (node_id, expression, status, details, parameter_ids). The engineStore holds values as Record<string, ValueData> keyed by cell_id, and constraints as Record<string, ConstraintData> keyed by node_id. The bridge module exports setParameter(cellId, value) for editing. selectionStore provides selectedEntity/hoveredEntity for cross-highlighting. Theme tokens are set as CSS custom properties (--reify-*). The project uses SolidJS with CSS modules, vitest + @solidjs/testing-library for testing. Tests mock the bridge module with vi.mock. The panels/ directory does not exist yet and must be created. All components are pure presentational SolidJS components that receive store state and callbacks as props (following the existing pattern of factory functions returning state + actions). Note: the task description mentions ValueData.kind and ConstraintData.label fields that don't exist in the actual types - we'll use the actual type definitions (determinacy for kind, details for label). The entity_path prefix (everything before the last dot) serves as the structure group key for PropertyEditor grouping. Entity paths follow the pattern 'StructureName.paramName' as seen in test data (e.g. 'Bracket.width').

prerequisites.description: Create gui/src/panels/ directory and gui/src/panels/index.ts barrel export with placeholder exports for all four components. Create empty CSS module files for each component.

steps.description: Write failing tests for PropertyEditor basic rendering in gui/src/__tests__/PropertyEditor.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='property-editor', (2) renders a search/filter input at top with placeholder text, (3) groups values by entity_path prefix showing structure name as collapsible group headers (e.g. entity_path 'Bracket.width' groups under 'Bracket'), (4) shows empty state message when no values provided

Implement PropertyEditor.tsx basic rendering and PropertyEditor.module.css: SolidJS component receiving values (Record<string, ValueData>), selectedEntity (string|null), and onSetParameter callback as props. Groups values by entity_path prefix (first dot-separated segment). Renders search input, collapsible group headers, and empty state. CSS uses --reify-* custom properties.

Write failing tests for PropertyEditor parameter rows: (1) each row displays name, formatted value, unit badge, and determinacy badge, (2) determinacy badge has correct data-determinacy attribute (determined/auto/constrained/undef) for styling, (3) determined params have editable input, auto/constrained/undef have read-only display, (4) editing a determined param value and pressing Enter calls onSetParameter(cellId, newValue)

Implement PropertyEditor parameter rows: render name, value (editable input for 'determined', read-only span for others), unit badge, determinacy badge with data-determinacy attribute. onKeyDown handler on input calls onSetParameter on Enter key. Determinacy badge colors via CSS: determined=--reify-success (green), auto=--reify-accent (blue), constrained=--reify-warning (yellow), undef=--reify-text-muted (gray).

Write failing tests for PropertyEditor interactive features: (1) collapse/expand: clicking a group header toggles visibility of child parameter rows, (2) search/filter: typing in filter input hides non-matching parameters and groups, (3) selection highlighting: when selectedEntity matches a structure group prefix, that group gets a 'selected' CSS class and auto-expands

Implement PropertyEditor interactivity: (1) collapsedGroups signal Set to track collapsed state, clicking header toggles, (2) filterText signal filters values by name substring match (case-insensitive), empty groups after filtering are hidden, (3) selectedEntity prop comparison against entity_path prefixes to add 'selected' class and force-expand matching group.

Write failing tests for ConstraintPanel basic rendering in gui/src/__tests__/ConstraintPanel.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='constraint-panel', (2) renders flat list of constraints, (3) each constraint row shows expression text and status badge, (4) status badges have correct data-status attribute: 'satisfied' (green checkmark), 'violated' (red X), other/indeterminate (gray ?), (5) constraints are sorted with violated first, then indeterminate, then satisfied, (6) empty state message when no constraints

Implement ConstraintPanel.tsx and ConstraintPanel.module.css: SolidJS component receiving constraints (Record<string, ConstraintData>) and values (Record<string, ValueData>) as props. Renders sorted flat list with status badges. Sort priority: violated=0, indeterminate=1, satisfied=2. Status badges use --reify-error (red), --reify-success (green), --reify-text-muted (gray) theme tokens.

Write failing tests for ConstraintPanel expansion: (1) clicking a violated constraint row expands it to show expression text and contributing parameter values (looked up via parameter_ids from values prop), (2) clicking again collapses it, (3) satisfied constraints are not expandable (no expand indicator), (4) expanded violated constraint shows each contributing parameter as 'name = value'

Implement ConstraintPanel expansion: expandedNodes signal Set tracks expanded node_ids. Click handler on violated/indeterminate rows toggles expansion. When expanded, render expression text and for each parameter_id look up ValueData from values prop to display 'name = value' entries. Satisfied rows have no expand affordance.

Write failing tests for Toolbar in gui/src/__tests__/Toolbar.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='toolbar', (2) renders an Export button with text 'Export', (3) renders a Fit to View button with text 'Fit to View', (4) clicking Export calls onExport callback, (5) clicking Fit to View calls onFitToView callback

Implement Toolbar.tsx and Toolbar.module.css: Simple SolidJS component with two buttons. Takes onExport and onFitToView callback props. Styled with --reify-accent for button backgrounds, --reify-surface-hover for hover states.

Write failing tests for StatusBar in gui/src/__tests__/StatusBar.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='status-bar', (2) displays evaluation phase text from evalStatus prop (e.g. 'idle', 'evaluating'), (3) displays triangle count computed from meshes (sum of indices.length/3 across all meshes), (4) displays constraint summary counts showing number satisfied, violated, and indeterminate

Implement StatusBar.tsx and StatusBar.module.css: Receives evalStatus (EvaluationStatus), meshes (Record<string, MeshData>), and constraints (Record<string, ConstraintData>) as props. Computes triangle count as sum of mesh.indices.length/3. Computes constraint summary by counting status values. Renders horizontal bar with phase, triangles, and constraint counts.

Write smoke integration test: import all four components from gui/src/panels/index.ts barrel export, render each with minimal valid props, verify all mount without errors and have their expected data-testid attributes
Finalize panels/index.ts barrel export with all four named exports. Run full test suite with 'vitest run' to verify all tests pass.

design_decisions.decision: Components receive data and callbacks as props rather than importing stores directly
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the existing codebase pattern where stores are created via factory functions (createEngineStore, createSelectionStore) and composed at the App level. Props make components testable without needing to set up global store context - tests can pass mock data directly.

design_decisions.decision: Entity path grouping uses the first dot-separated segment as structure name
design_decisions.rationale: ValueData.entity_path follows the pattern 'StructureName.paramName' (seen in test data: 'Bracket.width', 'Bracket.height'). Splitting on first '.' gives the structure group name naturally.

design_decisions.decision: Determinacy badge colors map to theme tokens: determined=--reify-success (green), auto=--reify-accent (blue), constrained=--reify-warning (yellow), undef=--reify-text-muted (gray)
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the task description color scheme and reuses existing theme tokens from theme.ts rather than introducing new colors.

design_decisions.decision: Constraint sort order: violated first, then indeterminate, then satisfied
design_decisions.rationale: Task specifies 'sort by status (violated first)'. Placing indeterminate between violated and satisfied provides a natural severity ordering for user attention.

design_decisions.decision: CSS modules pattern following existing App.module.css convention
design_decisions.rationale: The codebase already uses CSS modules (App.module.css) with --reify-* custom properties. Following the same pattern for consistency.

design_decisions.decision: Use the actual ConstraintData.details field (not 'label') for constraint display text
design_decisions.rationale: The task description mentions ConstraintData.label but the actual type in types.ts has 'details: string | null'. We follow the actual code, not the potentially stale task description.

design_decisions.decision: Triangle count computed from mesh indices.length / 3
design_decisions.rationale: Standard indexed triangle mesh convention where every 3 indices form one triangle. MeshData.indices is a Uint32Array of vertex indices.

design_decisions.decision: Use data-testid and data-* attributes for test selectors and state assertions
design_decisions.rationale: The existing test suite uses data-testid for component identification (App.test.tsx). Using data-determinacy and data-status attributes enables both CSS styling and test assertions without coupling to CSS class names.

reuse.how: Import directly for prop type annotations in all panel components
Components receive the store's state fields as props (values: Record<string, ValueData>, constraints: Record<string, ConstraintData>, evalStatus, meshes)
PropertyEditor receives selectedEntity prop from selection store for cross-highlighting
Passed as onSetParameter callback prop to PropertyEditor for inline editing. Signature: (cellId: string, value: string) => Promise<void>

All panel CSS modules reference --reify-* custom properties for consistent theming. Specific tokens: --reify-success, --reify-error, --reify-warning, --reify-accent, --reify-text-muted, --reify-panel-bg, --reify-border, --reify-surface-hover
Follow same test structure: import from vitest, render(() => <Component />), use screen.getByTestId and screen.getByText for assertions
StatusBar uses EvaluationStatus for phase display and MeshData for triangle count computation
Follow same conventions: var(--reify-*) with fallback values, .panel h3 style for headers, clean class naming
